summary: Superb soundboard capture of a heartfelt May 12 1981 show highlighted by an unforgettable He's Gone tribute and a soaring Althea.
review: The recording is praised for its crystal‑clear soundboard quality, delivering every nuance of the band’s performance. Musically the concert shines on He’s Gone, Althea, China > Rider and the tight L.L. Rain jam, though the first set suffers from a weary Jerry vocal and a lackluster Red Rooster. Overall listeners describe the show as emotionally charged and one of the stronger early‑'80s outings.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high star ratings (average ~4) and multiple reviewers praising both performance and soundboard quality, with only isolated criticism of the first set.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear, balanced mix with no reported drop‑outs; reviewers repeatedly note great or superb soundboard fidelity.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is solid but uneven, with some fatigue in vocals and a weak Red Rooster; the second half picks up dramatically, delivering powerful jams and seamless transitions.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for fiery guitar work (wah/mu‑tron), breath‑taking He's Gone, and overall on‑point playing after the first set; a few reviewers note tired vocals early on.
band_member_comments.Phil: Mentioned positively as locking in with Jerry during the second half.
band_member_comments.Bob: Vocals described as strained but holding up on Wharf Rat; generally solid rhythm guitar work.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (keyboards) lauded for his presence from the first note and tight playing, though one reviewer disliked a lite‑rock setting that dulled China Cat.
band_member_comments.Drums: Second‑half drumming called stellar; no negative remarks.
